personal.name: Saib Hossain
personal.objective: Motivated and detail-oriented Computer Science student with hands-on experience in Natural Language Processing (NLP), Transformer-based architectures, and fine-tuning large language models (LLMs). Proficient in applying machine learning and deep learning techniques to solve real-world problems, with a strong interest in advancing AI research. Seeking a Research Assistant role where I can contribute to optimizing NLP models, developing high-quality datasets, and supporting cutting-edge research initiatives. Passionate about continuous learning, collaboration, and driving meaningful innovation in artificial intelligence.

research_experience.title: Project: Correlation search between 'radiomic features' and lung tumors: stage, therapeutic success, survival time.
details: Developed deep learning pipelines for medical image analysis.
Extracted and analyzed radiomic features; supported model evaluation and statistical correlation studies.
Documented experiments and findings of model reliability, data bias, and clinical applicability, relevant to healthcare AI governance.

research_experience.title: Generative AI & Computer Vision
details: Designed a synthetic face generation pipeline with StyleGAN2 and Stable Diffusion XL (SDXL) to address data scarcity in vision research.
Fine-tuned diffusion models using LoRA for identity preservation and controlled diversity.
Applied Adaptive Discriminator Augmentation (ADA) to mitigate overfitting with small datasets.
Conducted error analysis and quality evaluation to improve generative consistency and realism.

research_experience.title: AI Safety & Alignment Research
details: Contributing to the Engineering & Research Division, focusing on the theoretical underpinnings of safe Artificial Intelligence systems.
Conducting critical literature reviews and drafting research briefs to support exploratory research into advanced model capabilities and alignment.
Synthesizing complex technical papers into actionable insights for ongoing R&D projects.

projects.title: Log Classification System using Hybrid NLP (Regex + ML + LLM)
projects.description: Developed a hybrid log classification system to accurately categorize synthetic and real-world system log messages using a combination of rule-based regex methods, traditional ML classifiers (Logistic Regression), and Large Language Models (LLMs).
details: Model: Regex rules for structured logs, Logistic Regression on MiniLM embeddings and DeepSeek LLaMA-70B via Groq API for ambiguous legacy logs.
Tech Stack: Python, Scikit-learn, Hugging Face Transformers, SentenceTransformers, BART, Pandas, Regex, Groq API
Key Features: Semantic clustering with DBSCAN and MiniLM, custom regex for precise classification, fallback logic pipeline (Regex → ML → LLM), instruction-tuned prompts.
Outcome: Achieved a modular, scalable, and explainable log classification system balancing speed, accuracy, and generality.

projects.title: End-to-End AI Project Portfolio
projects.description: A curated collection of end-to-end machine learning and deep learning projects showcasing core competencies across regression, classification, recommendation, NLP, and computer vision.
details: House Price Prediction: Used Linear Regression to predict housing prices. (Python, Scikit-learn)
Customer Churn Prediction: Built a classification model to identify high-risk churn users. (Scikit-learn, Random Forest)
Product Recommendation System: Designed a hybrid system combining SVD-based and content-based filtering. (Surprise Library, Scikit-learn)
Sentiment Analysis: Performed sentiment classification on customer reviews. (NLTK, Scikit-learn)
Object Detection for Inventory: Trained a YOLOv5 model to detect and localize products. (PyTorch, YOLOv5)

projects.title: DeepLearning-with-PyTorch
projects.description: Implemented various deep learning models and techniques using PyTorch, including CNNs, batch normalization, momentum optimization, and neural networks with different architectures.
details: Technologies: PyTorch, Python, Google Colab
Key Projects: CNN for image classification, Fashion MNIST, MNIST digit recognition, neural networks with hidden layers, momentum optimization, and anime image classification.

projects.title: AI-Powered Study Assistant Chatbot
projects.description: Built a smart chatbot for academic assistance using GPT-2 and custom Q&A datasets to simulate human-like tutoring interactions.
details: Model: Fine-tuned GPT-2 on domain-specific educational Q&A pairs.
Tech Stack: PyTorch, Hugging Face Transformers, FastAPI, JavaScript, HTML/CSS
Key Features: Custom dataset preparation, real-time communication via FastAPI, user-friendly web interface.
Outcome: Successfully deployed a responsive chatbot capable of assisting students with personalized answers.

projects.title: PyTorch vs TensorFlow
projects.description: Conducted a comparative analysis of PyTorch and TensorFlow frameworks focusing on deep learning model performance and development experience.
details: Models: Implemented CNN architectures in both frameworks for benchmarking.
Tech Stack: PyTorch, TensorFlow, NumPy, Matplotlib, Google Colab
Key Features: Identical architecture replication, evaluation of training speed and accuracy, visualization of metrics.
Outcome: Delivered an insightful comparison highlighting trade-offs in usability and performance.

projects.title: Book Recommendation System
projects.description: Built an intelligent Book Recommendation System leveraging Large Language Models (LLMs) to offer personalized suggestions through semantic analysis.
details: Model: Used OpenAI's GPT models to analyze book content.
Tech Stack: Hugging Face, React, Node.js, REST API, PyTorch
Key Features: Data collection from Kaggle, semantic embedding, interactive web UI.
Outcome: Responsive and accurate system that understands nuanced preferences.
